title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jeremy Fullerton discusses the disappearance of Olga Seminova with Hercule Poirot. Olga was a foreign nurse who cared for the late Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith, the aunt of Hugo Dray. Although Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith had originally intended her estate to pass to Hugo and his wife, a codicil written three weeks before her death diverted a significant portion of her wealth to Olga. However, handwriting experts determined the codicil was a forgery. It is noted that Olga had previously been tasked by Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith to imitate the woman's handwriting for private letters, and she attempted to use this skill to pass off the forged document. Following a lawsuit regarding the document's authenticity, Olga disappeared.

Fullerton reflects on his encounter with Olga two years prior, describing her as a passionate and resilient woman from Central Europe. He recalls a meeting where Olga sought his legal representation, claiming Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith had left her the money because the widow pitied her suffering and disliked her relatives. Olga argued that Mrs. Drake was overbearing and that her aunt deserved to enjoy her wealth. Olga defended her actions by claiming she was merely being kind to Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith by bringing her forbidden foods, such as chocolate and butter, despite medical advice. Fullerton refused to represent her, citing his existing duty to the Dray family.
